scope.summary:
Goal: Bring VS Code-class file-metadata segments to the existing WP-011 status/menu bar so the native code editor reaches VS Code parity on the language/EOL/indent/encoding/whitespace controls. The WP-011 top menu/status bar today (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/top_menu_bar.rs) already renders a fixed set of segments: layout name, active pane-type, line:column cursor position, swarm status, and theme. This MT ADDS five new interactive, editor-aware segments to the RIGHT cluster of that same bar — language mode, EOL, indent, file encoding, and a render-whitespace toggle — and the supporting detection logic. It MUST reuse the WP-011 status/menu-bar segment primitive in top_menu_bar.rs and the WP-011 right-click segment context-menu infrastructure in context_menu.rs / context_menu_surfaces.rs; it MUST NOT introduce a second status bar or new status-bar infra.

This MT builds on MT-001 (the code editor text buffer + extension-only language detection that already exists) and MT-010 (the editor document/model surface that owns per-document state). MT-001 today derives language only from the file extension; this MT upgrades that to content/shebang detection AND adds a user override that sticks per document. MT-010 is the document state owner, so the new metadata (language override, EOL, indent prefs, encoding, whitespace toggle) hangs off the document model MT-010 owns — do not invent a parallel document store.

Exact files to CREATE/EDIT (all under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/**):
- EDIT src/frontend/handshake_native/src/top_menu_bar.rs — add five new segment render functions to the existing right cluster, each built with the WP-011 status/menu-bar segment primitive (reuse the same StatusBarSegment / segment builder helper already in this file; do NOT define a new segment type). Each new segment: (1) renders a compact label reflecting the active editor document's metadata, (2) is interactive on left-click (opens its picker/menu), (3) wires a right-click context menu via the existing WP-011 segment context-menu helper in context_menu.rs / context_menu_surfaces.rs. The segments, left-to-right in the right cluster, are: LanguageMode, Eol, Indent, Encoding, RenderWhitespaceToggle. When no code-editor document is active (e.g. a non-code pane is focused), these five segments hide (return early) rather than render stale data.
- CREATE src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/language_mode.rs — the language-mode detection + override model. Defines: `LanguageId` (reuse the LanguageRegistry id type from MT-001 if already defined there; otherwise a thin newtype String), `LanguageDetection { detected: LanguageId, source: DetectionSource }` where `DetectionSource` is an enum `Extension | Shebang | Content | UserOverride`. Functions: `detect_language(path: Option<&str>, first_bytes: &[u8], full_text: &str) -> LanguageDetection` — first checks a user override on the document (highest precedence), then shebang (`#!` first line, e.g. `#!/usr/bin/env python` -> python, `#!/bin/bash` -> shell), then a small content heuristic table (e.g. leading `<?php`, `<!DOCTYPE html`, `{`/JSON shape, `package main` -> go) layered ABOVE the MT-001 extension-only path, then falls back to MT-001 extension detection. `available_languages() -> Vec<LanguageId>` returns the picker list (reuse MT-001 LanguageRegistry so the set matches the highlighter). `set_override(doc, lang)` records a per-document override on the MT-010 document model that survives re-render and re-focus.
- CREATE src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/file_meta.rs — the EOL / indent / encoding model. Defines: `Eol` enum `Lf | Crlf` with `detect(text: &str) -> Eol` (count `\r\n` vs lone `\n`; majority wins, default Lf) and `convert(buffer, target: Eol)` which rewrites all line endings in the document buffer as ONE undo step (use the MT-001/MT-010 buffer edit transaction API so a single Ctrl+Z reverts the whole conversion — do NOT emit per-line edits). `IndentStyle { kind: IndentKind, size: usize }` where `IndentKind` is `Tabs | Spaces`; `detect_indent(text: &str) -> IndentStyle` samples leading whitespace across lines (tabs present in leading indent -> Tabs; otherwise infer space size from the most common positive leading-space delta, default 4); `set_indent(doc, style)` updates the document's active editing indent behavior (Tab key inserts tab vs N spaces accordingly) on the MT-010 model. `Encoding` enum (at minimum `Utf8 | Utf8Bom | Utf16Le | Utf16Be`, default `Utf8`) with a display label and a `reopen_with(doc, encoding)` action that re-decodes the on-disk bytes (via the MT-010 document load path) under the chosen encoding — encoding detection on load reads a BOM if present, else assumes Utf8. `RenderWhitespace` is a bool toggle stored on the document/editor view state; when on, the code editor renders middots for spaces and arrows for tabs (the actual glyph rendering lives in the MT-001 editor draw path — this MT only owns the toggle state + status-bar control; flip the flag the MT-001 draw path reads).

Exact crates / egui / AccessKit patterns:
- Segment rendering reuses the existing WP-011 status/menu-bar segment helper in top_menu_bar.rs (whatever it is named there — e.g. `fn segment(ui, label, on_click) -> Response`); call it five more times, do not re-implement hover/padding/separator styling.
- Pickers: the language-mode picker and encoding picker open as a small egui popup anchored to the clicked segment using `egui::popup::popup_below_widget` (or the WP-011 segment popup helper if one exists), listing options as selectable rows; EOL and Indent use the same popup pattern (EOL: two rows LF/CRLF; Indent: rows for Tabs and Spaces 2/4/8). RenderWhitespace is a direct toggle on click (no popup) plus a checkbox in its right-click menu.
- Right-click context menus reuse the WP-011 segment context-menu infrastructure in context_menu.rs / context_menu_surfaces.rs: attach via the same helper WP-011 segments already use (e.g. `response.context_menu(|ui| { ... })`), exposing quick actions (e.g. on the EOL segment: 'Convert to LF', 'Convert to CRLF'; on Indent: 'Convert indentation to tabs/spaces', 'Change tab size'; on Encoding: 'Reopen with Encoding', 'Save with Encoding' if MT-010 exposes a save path, else only Reopen).
- AccessKit: give each new segment a stable author_id so swarm agents can address it — `status-bar-language-mode`, `status-bar-eol`, `status-bar-indent`, `status-bar-encoding`, `status-bar-render-whitespace`, each role=Button; each popup list item author_id `status-bar-{segment}-item-{value}` role=ListItem; reuse the WP-011 AccessKit id-registry helper in accessibility/ (accessibility/registry.rs / accessibility/mod.rs) (do NOT hand-roll a new registry). Picker popups expose role=List on the container.

Exact handshake-native API bound: NONE backend. This MT is pure frontend status/menu-bar + editor-state work. Language/EOL/indent detection are in-process string analysis; encoding reopen routes through the EXISTING MT-010 document load path (frontend), not a new backend call. If a needed document-model field (override slot, indent prefs, whitespace flag, encoding) does not yet exist on the MT-010 model, extend the MT-010 frontend model struct (allowed under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/**); do NOT add a backend API or a backend rewrite — any genuine backend gap is a typed blocker, not an implementation here.

Step-by-step:
1. In file_meta.rs implement `Eol`, `IndentStyle`, `Encoding`, `RenderWhitespace` plus their detect/convert/set functions; unit-test detection and the single-undo EOL convert in isolation.
2. In language_mode.rs implement `DetectionSource`, `detect_language` (override -> shebang -> content -> extension fallback), `available_languages`, `set_override`; unit-test that shebang/content beat extension and that a user override sticks per document.
3. In top_menu_bar.rs, locate the existing right-cluster render block; add the five segments using the existing segment helper, each reading the active editor document's metadata via MT-010, each with left-click picker/toggle and right-click context menu via the WP-011 context_menu helpers, each with the AccessKit author_id above; hide all five when no code-editor document is active.
4. Wire the language-mode override and indent/EOL/encoding/whitespace mutations back onto the MT-010 document model so they persist across re-render and re-focus and so the MT-001 editor draw + Tab-key behavior reads the updated state.
5. Add the egui_kittest test that the status/menu bar shows the language/EOL/indent/encoding segments with working right-click menus.

Dependencies / unblock conditions: MT-001 (text buffer + extension-only language detection + editor draw path) and MT-010 (document/model surface) must be merged first. Blocks MT-072.
Risk if missed: Language mode, EOL, indent, and encoding controls are absent from the status bar; the native editor has a VS Code parity gap on file metadata (users cannot see or change line endings, indent style, encoding, or override the detected language).
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: Language detection by shebang and content overrides MT-001 extension-only detection — proven by `cargo test -p handshake-native`: a file whose extension says one language but whose shebang/content says another resolves to the shebang/content language (DetectionSource = Shebang or Content), and a user override (DetectionSource = UserOverride) beats all auto-detection and sticks per document across re-render.

AC-002: EOL convert rewrites line endings LF<->CRLF as exactly one undo step — proven by `cargo test -p handshake-native`: convert a multi-line buffer, assert all endings changed, then a single undo restores the original buffer exactly.

AC-003: Indent detection picks tabs vs spaces and infers size — proven by `cargo test -p handshake-native`: tab-indented sample yields IndentKind::Tabs; 4-space sample yields Spaces size=4; a set_indent override updates the document's Tab-key editing behavior.

AC-004: The status/menu bar renders five editor segments (language mode, EOL, indent, encoding, render-whitespace) reflecting the active document, each with a working right-click context menu — proven by egui_kittest; segments are built with the WP-011 segment primitive in top_menu_bar.rs (no new status-bar infra).
AC-005: All five segments hide when no code-editor document is active and re-appear with correct metadata when a code document is focused — proven by egui_kittest.

AC-006: AccessKit nodes present with stable author_ids 'status-bar-language-mode', 'status-bar-eol', 'status-bar-indent', 'status-bar-encoding', 'status-bar-render-whitespace' (role=Button) — proven by AccessKit tree dump in egui_kittest.
AC-007: `cargo test -p handshake-native` passes with no failures across the new language_mode and file_meta tests.

AC-008: No new status-bar segment type or second status bar introduced — proven by grep showing the new segments call the existing WP-011 segment helper in top_menu_bar.rs and existing context-menu helper in context_menu.rs / context_menu_surfaces.rs.
proof_targets: PT-001: `cargo test -p handshake-native` language detection by shebang/content overrides extension-only detection; user override sticks per document => all tests PASS
PT-002: `cargo test -p handshake-native` EOL convert LF<->CRLF rewrites line endings as one undo step => single undo restores original, test PASS
PT-003: `cargo test -p handshake-native` indent detect picks tabs vs spaces + size; override updates editing behavior => test PASS
PT-004: egui_kittest (`cargo test -p handshake-native`) status/menu bar shows language/EOL/indent/encoding segments with right-click menus => interaction test PASS
PT-005: AccessKit tree snapshot asserts the five status-bar segment author_ids present with role=Button
implementation_notes: Cross-WP dependency: this MT reuses WP-KERNEL-011 deliverables — the status/menu-bar segment primitive in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/top_menu_bar.rs, the context-menu helpers in context_menu.rs / context_menu_surfaces.rs, and the AccessKit id-registry in accessibility/ — which must already be merged from WP-011 before this MT runs.
Reuse the existing WP-011 status/menu-bar segment helper in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/top_menu_bar.rs verbatim for all five new segments; do not duplicate hover/padding/separator styling.

Right-click menus must use the same WP-011 segment context-menu helper the existing layout/theme segments use (context_menu.rs / context_menu_surfaces.rs, e.g. response.context_menu) — do not hand-roll a new menu system.
Detection precedence in detect_language is strict: UserOverride > Shebang > Content > Extension(MT-001 fallback). Encode this order explicitly; never let extension win over an override or shebang.
EOL convert MUST be a single buffer edit transaction so one Ctrl+Z reverts it; use the MT-001/MT-010 buffer transaction API rather than emitting per-line edits.
Hang language override, indent prefs, encoding, and the render-whitespace flag off the MT-010 document model — do not create a parallel document/state store.
RenderWhitespace here owns only the toggle state and the status-bar control; the actual middot/arrow glyph rendering is read from this flag by the MT-001 editor draw path.
available_languages() must source from the MT-001 LanguageRegistry so the picker set matches the highlighter's supported languages exactly.

Encoding reopen routes through the existing MT-010 document load path (re-decode on-disk bytes under the chosen encoding); read a BOM on load if present, else assume Utf8. No backend call — backend gaps are typed blockers.
AccessKit ids are registered via the WP-011 registry in accessibility/registry.rs (accessibility/mod.rs); do not hand-roll a new id registry.
ports_from_react: WP-011 status/menu-bar segments (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/top_menu_bar.rs) — segment render pattern, right-cluster layout, and the segment context-menu pattern (context_menu.rs / context_menu_surfaces.rs) reused as the substrate for the five new editor metadata segments
binds_backend_api: NONE — pure frontend status/menu-bar + editor-state work; language/EOL/indent detection are in-process; encoding reopen routes through the existing MT-010 frontend document load path; any genuine backend document-metadata gap is a typed blocker, not implemented here (no backend rewrite, PostgreSQL/EventLedger only at the backend, never SQLite)

risks: RISK-001: Adding a parallel status bar or a new segment type instead of reusing the WP-011 segment primitive in top_menu_bar.rs — fragments the shell and breaks the single-status-bar invariant.
RISK-002: EOL conversion emitted as per-line edits instead of one transaction — a single undo only reverts the last line, corrupting the document and the undo history.
RISK-003: Detection precedence wrong — extension detection (MT-001) silently overrides a user's explicit language override or a shebang, so the user's choice does not stick.

RISK-004: Storing the new metadata (override/indent/encoding/whitespace) in a side store instead of the MT-010 document model — state desyncs from the document, is lost on re-focus, and the editor draw/Tab-key path reads stale values.
RISK-005: Encoding reopen attempts a new backend call or rewrites the load path — violates the no-backend-rewrite constraint; mis-detected encoding garbles non-UTF8 files.

RISK-006: Segments render stale metadata (or AccessKit author_id collisions) when a non-code pane is focused or multiple editor docs exist — confuses users and swarm agents addressing the wrong segment.
RISK-007: Indent detection mis-infers size on mixed tabs/spaces files, flipping the Tab key to the wrong behavior mid-edit.
minimum_controls: MC-001: Grep-gate that the five segments call the existing WP-011 segment helper in top_menu_bar.rs and existing context-menu helper in context_menu.rs / context_menu_surfaces.rs; reject any new segment type or second status-bar construct.
MC-002: Unit test that EOL convert is a single undo step (one Ctrl+Z restores the original buffer byte-for-byte).
MC-003: Unit test the strict precedence UserOverride > Shebang > Content > Extension, including the case where extension and override disagree.
MC-004: Assert (test or review) that override/indent/encoding/whitespace are read from and written to the MT-010 document model, not a separate store; verify persistence across simulated re-focus.
MC-005: Encoding reopen must route through the existing MT-010 load path; any missing backend capability is filed as a typed blocker, not coded around. No SQLite anywhere.
MC-006: All five segments return early (hide) when no code-editor document is active; AccessKit author_ids are stable and unique per segment; verify via egui_kittest AccessKit dump.
MC-007: Default indent to Spaces size=4 and default EOL to LF and default Encoding to Utf8 when detection is ambiguous, to avoid surprising mid-edit Tab-key flips.
